summary: Decompressive surgery is considered potentially beneficial but lacks definitive evidence specifically for spinal cord sarcoidosis with compressive cervical myelopathy; its classification outside standard surgical interventions limits confidence in established efficacy. The available literature does not provide sufficient outcome-based validation to confirm consistent benefits across patients with this condition.
